sanskrit: अपाने जुह्वति प्राणं प्राणेऽपानं तथापरे। प्राणापानगती रुद्ध्वा प्राणायामपरायणाः। अपरे नियताहाराः प्राणान्प्राणेषु जुह्वति।।
transliteration: apāne juhvati prāṇaṃ prāṇe 'pānaṃ tathāpare | prāṇāpāna-gatī ruddhvā prāṇāyāma-parāyaṇāḥ | apare niyatāhārāḥ prāṇān prāṇeṣu juhvati ||
literal_meaning: Some offer the outgoing breath into the downward breath, others the downward into the outgoing; blocking the flow of both they remain dedicated to breath-control; still others, restraining diet, sacrifice the life-breath into the life-breath itself.
contextual_meaning: Krishna lists advanced breath-control techniques as legitimate yogic sacrifices that quiet the nervous system and focus attention, but hints that without devotion they remain technical rather than liberating.
psychological_state: Hyper-controlled, hyper-focused state akin to clinical breath-work or bio-feedback training—useful for down-regulating threat-response yet risking obsessive self-monitoring.
trigger_scenario: A high-performing professional adopts intermittent fasting, cold-exposure, and box-breathing apps to master stress, but secretly measures self-worth by heart-rate-variability scores.
anchor_text: Let every breath be offered as love, not merely measured for control.
